skill.name: nextjs-15-patterns
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through Next.js 15 App Router patterns, Server Components, Server Actions, data fetching, routing, caching, and metadata.
skill.summary: Apply modern Next.js 15 App Router patterns with practical examples.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: The high static score is driven by false positives in Markdown documentation. Backticks are TypeScript code fences and template literals, not Ruby shell execution, and the fetch calls are Next.js examples using example.com or local routes. No prompt injection, executable scripts, malicious network behavior, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are fenced TypeScript examples, route diagrams, inline file names, or a TypeScript metadata template literal. They do not invoke a shell, spawn a process, or execute user-controlled commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown references and the marked lines are code fences, table entries, or TypeScript syntax. No executable Ruby, shell command, child process API, or command injection path is present.

low_findings.title: Sample Fetch Calls Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are sample Next.js fetch patterns that use api.example.com or local /api/users routes. They are not executed by the skill and do not transmit secrets or user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The calls appear inside Markdown TypeScript examples for caching and TanStack Query usage. The external URL is example.com, and no credential, environment variable, or hidden endpoint is involved.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to a description field and a TypeScript metadata description property. The reconnaissance finding points to a normal Server Component example, not host or system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive metadata, a component function signature, and a page metadata description. They do not contain cryptographic algorithms, filesystem probing, environment probing, or system commands.

content.value_statement: Next.js 15 projects need correct App Router, Server Component, and Server Action patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise reference examples for modern implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Server Components and Client Components.
Shows Server Action patterns with validation, revalidation, and redirects.
Summarizes App Router file conventions, dynamic routes, and intercepting routes.
Provides data fetching patterns for parallel, sequential, cached, and streamed data.
Includes route handler and metadata examples for common pages and APIs.
limitations: Does not run Next.js commands, install packages, or modify projects by itself.
Examples are concise and may need adaptation for a specific database or auth system.
Does not cover every Next.js 15 feature or migration scenario.
Does not replace official documentation for version-specific edge cases.

use_cases.title: Create App Router Pages
use_cases.description: Generate pages, layouts, loading states, and error boundaries that follow Next.js 15 conventions.

use_cases.title: Implement Server Actions
use_cases.description: Add mutation flows with input validation, cache revalidation, and optional redirects.

use_cases.title: Review Data Fetching Choices
use_cases.description: Compare server fetching, client fetching, caching, streaming, and sequential loading patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Explain The Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how I should structure a basic Next.js 15 App Router page with loading and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Server Action
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Server Action plan for a form that validates input, writes data, and refreshes the affected route.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature idea and recommend server fetching, client fetching, caching, or streaming for each data source.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor A Route Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Next.js 15 route structure with route groups, dynamic segments, metadata, and an intercepted modal route.

output_examples.input: Ask for a user profile page pattern.
output_examples.output: A concise plan that uses a Server Component, colocated data fetching, a not-found state, metadata generation, and a loading boundary.

output_examples.input: Ask how to handle a create-post form.
output_examples.output: A Server Action approach with schema validation, database mutation, cache revalidation, structured errors, and an optional redirect.

output_examples.input: Ask for data fetching guidance for a dashboard.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to fetch independent data in parallel, stream slow sections with Suspense, and cache stable API responses.

best_practices: Prefer Server Components unless browser state, effects, or event handlers are required.
Validate all Server Action inputs before database writes or cache revalidation.
Fetch data where it is used and stream slower sections with Suspense.
anti_patterns: Do not mark a whole route as client-side when only one interactive component needs it.
Do not use API route mutations when a Server Action is simpler and appropriate.
Do not ignore loading, error, and not-found states in App Router routes.

faq.question: Does this skill support Next.js 15?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on Next.js 15 App Router patterns and related React 19 Server Action usage.

faq.question: Can it migrate an entire project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and guidance that an AI assistant can apply during project edits.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It includes input validation examples for Server Actions, but it is not a full application security checklist.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only. Sample fetch calls are examples for application code.

faq.question: What projects benefit most?
faq.answer: It is most useful for React teams building or reviewing Next.js App Router features.
